hypothesis: Energy ETF proxies may be worth scarce slot competition only when their own signal-day state confirms relative strength or near-high momentum.
why_tested: exp-20260428-035 rejected simple ETF expansion but identified Energy/USO late-window upside and required a state discriminator for any valid retry.

rejection_reason: Energy ETF state filters did not solve the slot displacement problem across fixed windows.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry Energy ETF universe expansion with only self-OHLCV state predicates.
A valid retry needs an orthogonal event/state source, such as energy breadth, fresh commodity news, or production watchlist parity.
If a shadow result is promoted later, implement it in shared production/backtest policy first.
